description: Harness engineering hooks — enforce bootstrap gate, WIP=1, verification before completion, and clean state at session end.

hooks.prompt: HARNESS CLOCK-IN: Before any other action, (1) read AGENTS.md, (2) read progress.md, (3) read feature_list.json, (4) review git log --oneline -5, (5) run ./init.sh. Announce understanding before proceeding. If ./init.sh fails, fix the harness before working on features.

BOOTSTRAP GATE: Before writing or editing code files, verify the four harness files exist on disk: AGENTS.md, feature_list.json, progress.md, and init.sh. If any are missing, create them first using the minimal-pack templates. Do not write application code until the bootstrap gate passes.

EXPLORE BEFORE CODE: Before writing new code, HAVE YOU: (1) searched the codebase for existing types (Grep for class/type definitions)? (2) checked for existing functions that do 80%+ of what you need? (3) read the relevant files in the module you're about to modify? (4) built a mental Type Inventory? Never write a function that duplicates an existing one. Never use `Any` when a real type exists in the codebase. EXPLORE → READ → THEN WRITE.

PRE-COMMIT CHECK: Before `git commit`, confirm: (1) verification ran fresh this session, (2) zero failures in output, (3) evidence recorded in feature_list.json. Run `./init.sh` first if verification hasn't run in this session.

PRE-PUSH CHECK: Before `git push`, confirm: (1) `./init.sh` passes with zero failures, (2) progress.md is updated, (3) feature_list.json feature states are accurate, (4) all work is committed with descriptive messages.

HARNESS CLOCK-OUT: Before the session ends, run the exit checklist: (1) ./init.sh passes, (2) progress.md updated, (3) feature_list.json updated, (4) all work committed, (5) no debug artifacts/print()/TODOs, (6) no temp files, (7) standard startup works, (8) next session ready. Leave a clean restart path.
